skill.name: seo-copywriter-pro
skill.description: Creates SEO-focused page plans, copy templates, schema guidance, keyword maps, and batch content workflows for local service websites.
skill.summary: Generate local SEO content systems, page outlines, metadata, and validation workflows.
skill.author: 1bertogit

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-severity patterns, but most Markdown backtick, weak crypto, C2 keyword, and prompt-like signals are false positives from documentation and examples. The confirmed risks are legitimate but sensitive SEO tooling behaviors: URL crawling, optional PageSpeed API-key use, recursive local file reads, and user-controlled file writes.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Network Crawling and API-Key Use
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The SEO auditor fetches robots.txt, sitemap.xml, crawled pages, and Google PageSpeed data for a user-supplied target. This matches the tool purpose, but it can make outbound requests and can send an optional API key to Google PageSpeed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The requests are direct and semantically confirmed by the SEO audit workflow. Confidence is not higher because the endpoint and API-key use are documented and legitimate, not covert exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled File Writes
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The batch generator writes page files to output paths supplied by the configuration, and the auditor writes reports to the --save path. This is expected functionality, but untrusted configs or arguments can overwrite files the user can access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write paths are controlled by user input and are not constrained to a safe directory. The behavior is useful for the skill, so this is a misuse risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Recursive Local File Reading
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The auditor recursively scans local project files under the target path and reads page-like files for SEO analysis. This is legitimate, but users should run it only on intended project directories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file traversal and read behavior is explicit and required by the local audit feature. The risk depends on user-selected paths, and I found no evidence of stealthy collection.

low_findings.title: Documentation Command and Backtick False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static external-command alerts in SKILL.md and reference Markdown are from fenced examples, inline code, page templates, and keyword lists. I did not find evidence that these Markdown snippets execute automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sampled hits are clearly Markdown content or templates. No runtime shell invocation is present in those locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and C2 Keyword False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak-cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary marketing or template text, and the C2 keyword alert is a CSS class named score-poor. No evidence found of cryptographic code or command-and-control behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed C2 alert is visibly CSS styling, and sampled weak-crypto locations are non-code content. Confidence is slightly reduced because not every repeated wording hit was individually reviewed.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Examples Are Public Analytics IDs
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW. The Astro reference shows PUBLIC_GA4_ID and PUBLIC_GTM_ID usage for analytics. These are client-exposed public identifiers, but users should avoid placing private secrets in similar public variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented variables are public analytics identifiers, not secret keys. The pattern still deserves a warning because public environment variables are easy to misuse.

dangerous_patterns.title: Outbound HTTP Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The auditor can fetch user-supplied URLs and Google PageSpeed API data. This should be disclosed before use in restricted network environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are direct requests.get calls. The purpose is legitimate SEO auditing, not hidden exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unrestricted Output Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated pages and reports can be written to paths supplied by command arguments or config files. Users should review paths before running scripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The path control is explicit in the scripts. The issue is bounded by local user permissions and normal CLI usage.

content.value_statement: Local SEO teams need many useful pages without losing structure or search intent. This skill provides page templates, keyword workflows, schema guidance, and validation checks.
actual_capabilities: Maps local SEO keywords by service, location, problem, and authority intent.
Creates structured service pages, location pages, blog articles, FAQs, and metadata.
Provides Astro-focused SEO guidance, schema examples, sitemap setup, and analytics snippets.
Includes scripts for batch page generation and SEO checklist validation.
Audits local or live sites for SEO metadata, schema, links, robots.txt, sitemap, and performance signals.
Supports multilingual planning with hreflang and culturally adapted translation guidance.
limitations: Ranking outcomes are not guaranteed and depend on competition, authority, backlinks, and indexing.
Generated medical, legal, or regulated claims need expert review before publication.
Scripts can read project files, crawl URLs, and write outputs when users run them.
The workflow is strongest for local service sites and may need adaptation for ecommerce or media sites.

use_cases.title: Launch Local Service Pages
use_cases.description: Build keyword maps and page briefs for many city, neighborhood, and service combinations.

use_cases.title: Create Specialist Authority Content
use_cases.description: Draft educational pages with structured headings, FAQs, schema guidance, and cautious claims.

use_cases.title: Validate SEO Implementation
use_cases.description: Check created pages for metadata length, H1 structure, internal links, image alt text, and FAQ coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Page Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an SEO page brief for [service] in [location]. Include search intent, URL slug, title, description, H1, H2s, FAQ questions, and CTA.

prompt_templates.title: Service Page Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a service page for [primary keyword]. Use the skill tone guide, include secondary keywords, add FAQ content, and keep claims evidence-based.

prompt_templates.title: Batch Content Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30-page local SEO plan for [business type]. Group pages by service, location, problem, and authority intent. Include priority and internal links.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Technical SEO Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Astro SEO implementation for metadata, schema, sitemap, robots.txt, hreflang, internal linking, and Core Web Vitals readiness. Prioritize fixes.

output_examples.input: A clinic needs a page for facial surgery in Sao Paulo.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a keyword-focused brief with URL slug, metadata, H1, section plan, FAQ topics, schema recommendation, and CTA direction.

output_examples.input: An agency wants 50 local service pages for plumbing services.
output_examples.output: The skill organizes pages by emergency, service, location, and problem keywords, then proposes internal links and batch generation fields.

output_examples.input: A developer asks whether new pages meet the SEO checklist.
output_examples.output: The skill highlights title length, description length, H1 count, content depth, internal links, image alt text, FAQ coverage, and schema gaps.

best_practices: Start with verified services, locations, proof points, and claim limits before generating pages.
Review every generated page for accuracy, local relevance, and duplicated language.
Run scripts only on trusted projects and review output paths before writing files.
anti_patterns: Do not publish medical, legal, or financial claims without qualified human review.
Do not generate hundreds of near-identical pages with only location names changed.
Do not run crawlers or batch writers against untrusted targets or unreviewed configuration files.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates SEO briefs, page drafts, keyword maps, metadata, FAQ structures, schema guidance, and validation workflows.

faq.question: Does it guarantee Google rankings?
faq.answer: No. It supports sound SEO execution, but rankings depend on many external factors.

faq.question: Can it generate many pages at once?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a batch generation script and planning guidance for large local SEO projects.

faq.question: Is it only for Astro sites?
faq.answer: No. The strongest technical examples use Astro, but the copy and SEO planning can apply to other stacks.

faq.question: Does it access the network?
faq.answer: Only if users run the included auditor against live URLs or PageSpeed. Review targets and API-key use first.

faq.question: Who should review the final copy?
faq.answer: A subject-matter expert should review accuracy, compliance, claims, and local business details before publication.
